2026-04-22T19:25:34.2521761Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T19:25:34.2522175Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T19:25:34.2522530Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T19:25:34.2522828Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:25:34.2523055Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-22T19:28:38.3650091Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3650576Z   1. Added `PlatformHeaders` static class - referenced in CHANGELOG but NOT mentioned in the README's Key Concepts section
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3651331Z   2. Added `RequestIdMiddleware` - referenced in CHANGELOG but NOT in README Key Concepts
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3651907Z   3. Added `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` - not mentioned in README at all
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3652567Z   4. Breaking change: replaced 6 individual extension methods with `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()`
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3653609Z   5. Breaking change: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` / `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` - use `PlatformHeaders` instead
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3654525Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3654704Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3658189Z   6. The Key Concepts section covers AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder, AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, and Health endpoint, but it's missing documentation for the new PlatformHeaders class, RequestIdMiddleware, the x-request-id header behavior, and the updated Tier 3 setup pattern - though the Troubleshooting section does already mention the new `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` methods. So the real issue is that `PlatformHeaders`, `RequestIdMiddleware`, and `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` are all public APIs documented in the CHANGELOG, but they're missing from the README's Key Concepts section—especially problematic since `PlatformHeaders` is essential for developers building Tier 3 setups with `IsolationContext`. The README changes were minimal, so these important new features aren't surfaced to users at all.
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3662042Z 
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3662629Z ✓ github-get_commit(include_diff: true, owner: Azure, page: 2, repo: azure-sdk-for-net, ...)
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3663161Z 
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3664906Z ◆ Let me look at the README diff directly in a different way. I know the README only had 1 addition and 1 deletion. Let me read the current README for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` and compare it with what the CHANGELOG says about the new features.
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3666580Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3667104Z   Looking at the README content I already have, and the CHANGELOG:
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3667578Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3667779Z   The README currently covers:
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3668174Z   - Getting started (install, prerequisites, start a server with code snippet)
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3669022Z   - Key concepts: AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder (mentions x-platform-server version header), AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, Health endpoint
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3669667Z   - Examples (link to samples)
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3669941Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3670096Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3670482Z   - Troubleshooting (Port already in use, Logging - mentions AddAgentServerCore/UseAgentServerCore)
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3670975Z   - Next steps, Contributing
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3671208Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3673001Z   Now I'm comparing this against the beta.23 changes to identify what's not documented. The `PlatformHeaders` class is a new public type that centralizes header name constants, which is important for users working with IsolationContext-based features. The `RequestIdMiddleware` and x-request-id header behavior aren't documented anywhere, which is a gap since this is critical for request debugging and correlation. There's also `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` that's not mentioned, though it's likely internal enough to skip.
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3674853Z   
2026-04-22T19:28:38.3676778Z   The most significant issue is that the README doesn't warn about the breaking change—users need to know they should use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead of the removed `IsolationContext` constants. I should file an issue focused on the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package since that's where these public types were introduced.

Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). The response exists in storage\\nand must be cleaned up on DELETE — not just evicted from tracker.\\n\\n- Attempt best-effort provider.DeleteResponseAsync (ignore NotFound)\\n- Attempt best-effort stream provider.DeleteEventStreamAsync\\n- Return standard 200 + DeleteResponseResult JSON (not 204)\\n- Add test: Background_UpdateFailure_DeleteCleansUpStorageAndTracker\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n---------\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\",\"author\":{\"name\":\"Ravi Pidaparthi\",\"email\":\"rapida@microsoft.com\",\"date\":\"2026-04-22T19:04:19Z\"},\"committer\":{\"name\":\"GitHub\",\"email\":\"noreply@github.com\",\"date\":\"2026-04-22T19:04:19Z\"}},\"author\":{\"login\":\"RaviPidaparthi\",\"id\":28370364,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/RaviPidaparthi\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28370364?v=4\"},\"committer\":{\"login\":\"web-flow\",\"id\":19864447,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/web-flow\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4\"}},{\"sha\":\"[REDACTED]\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]\",\"commit\":{\"message\":\"Baseline existing projects on NUnit 3 and upgrade the repo to NUnit 4 (#58316)\\n\\n* Upgrade central NUnit to 4.4.0, pin existing projects to NUnit 3\\n\\n- Update NUnitVersion from 3.14.0 to 4.4.0 in Directory.Packages.props\\n- Update NUnit3TestAdapter from 4.6.0 to 6.1.0 in Directory.Support.Packages.props\\n- Add NUnit 3.14.0 + NUnit3TestAdapter 4.6.0 overrides for 130 existing\\n  test project families to preserve current behavior\\n- Clean up redundant NUnit 4 overrides from 6 packages already migrated\\n- Pin Playwright library NUnit references to 3.14.0 (were using variable)\\n\\nNew projects will default to NUnit 4.4.0. This caused\\nNU1605 package downgrade errors in CI.\\n\\nFix: remove the adapter version pins. These packages will now use the\\ncentral NUnit3TestAdapter 6.1.0. Deleted empty override files for\\nInvocations and Projects packages.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Revert NUnit3TestAdapter to 4.6.0 to fix CI failures\\n\\nNUnit3TestAdapter 6.1.0 caused two classes of CI failures:\\n- CS8892: The adapter's Microsoft Testing Platform generates an entry\\n  point that conflicts with BenchmarkDotNet's Program.Main() in perf\\n  projects\\n- TearDown failures: The adapter 6.1.0 changes test lifecycle behavior,\\n  causing recording/playback session errors in RM test projects still\\n  on NUnit 3.14.0\\n\\nReverting to 4.6.0 which supports both NUnit 3 and NUnit 4 (since\\n4.5.0). The adapter can be upgraded to 6.x later once all projects\\nare migrated to NUnit 4.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Restore NUnit3TestAdapter 5.1.0 overrides for NUnit 4 AI packages\\n\\nThese 5 packages use Microsoft.Testing.Platform APIs that require\\nNUnit3TestAdapter 5.1.0+.
